extra.reasoning:
The property requires that setting fever=true (other inputs unchanged) never lowers acuity. It is violated because r1 (IF fever THEN acuity := self_care) has the highest priority (100) and maps fever to the LOWEST acuity level (self_care), so whenever fever becomes true it overrides every other rule and the default, forcing acuity down.

Concrete counterexample (the witness assignment is the fever=true state; compare against the same inputs with fever=false):
- Baseline fever=false, leg_swelling=false, abdominal_pain=false: no rule matches, default applies -> acuity = routine.
- After setting fever=true, leg_swelling=false, abdominal_pain=false: r1 matches (priority 100, only match) -> acuity = self_care.
Since self_care < routine in the listed enum order [self_care, routine, urgent, emergent], setting fever true LOWERED acuity, violating monotonicity.

An even more dramatic violation exists at leg_swelling=true, abdominal_pain=false: fever=false yields emergent (r3), while fever=true makes r1 (priority 100) beat r3 (priority 3), yielding self_care -- a drop from the highest to the lowest acuity. The reported witness uses the simplest all-false baseline.
